summary.text: Provides extension methods for adding the Azure Managed Redis resources to the application model.

members.name: AddAzureManagedRedis
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzureManagedRedisResource> AzureManagedRedisExtensions.AddAzureManagedRedis(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name)

summary.text: Adds an Azure Managed Redis resource to the application model.

remarks.text: By default, the Azure Managed Redis resource is configured to use Microsoft Entra ID (Azure Active Directory) for authentication. This requires changes to the application code to use an azure credential to authenticate with the resource. See https://github.com/Azure/Microsoft.Azure.StackExchangeRedis for more information.
The following example creates an Azure Managed Redis resource and referencing that resource in a .NET project.

builder.text: The builder for the distributed application.

members.name: RunAsContainer
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzureManagedRedisResource> AzureManagedRedisExtensions.RunAsContainer(this IResourceBuilder<AzureManagedRedisResource> builder, Action<IResourceBuilder<RedisResource>>? configureContainer = null)

summary.text: Configures an Azure Managed Redis resource to run locally in a container.

remarks.text: The following example creates an Azure Managed Redis resource that runs locally in a Redis container and referencing that resource in a .NET project.

builder.text: The Azure Managed Redis resource builder.

configureContainer.text: Callback that exposes underlying container to allow for customization.

summary.text: Configures the resource to use access key authentication for Azure Managed Redis.

remarks.text: The following example creates an Azure Managed Redis resource that uses access key authentication.

builder.text: The Azure Managed Redis resource builder.

summary.text: Configures the resource to use access key authentication for Azure Managed Redis.

builder.text: The Azure Managed Redis resource builder.

keyVaultBuilder.text: The Azure Key Vault resource builder where the connection string used to connect to this AzureManagedRedisResource will be stored.

summary.text: Provides extension methods for adding the Azure Redis resources to the application model.

members.name: AddAzureRedis
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzureRedisCacheResource> AzureRedisExtensions.AddAzureRedis(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name)

attributes.name: System.ObsoleteAttribute
constructorArguments: This method is obsolete and will be removed in a future version. Use AddAzureManagedRedis instead which provisions Azure Managed Redis.

summary.text: Adds an Azure Cache for Redis resource to the application model.

remarks.text: By default, the Azure Cache for Redis resource is configured to use Microsoft Entra ID (Azure Active Directory) for authentication. This requires changes to the application code to use an azure credential to authenticate with the resource. See https://github.com/Azure/Microsoft.Azure.StackExchangeRedis for more information. You can use the
method to configure the resource to use access key authentication.
The following example creates an Azure Cache for Redis resource and referencing that resource in a .NET project.

summary.text: Gets the connection string template for the manifest for the Azure Managed Redis resource.
Gets the host name for the Redis server.

remarks.text: In container mode, resolves to the container's primary endpoint host and port. In Azure mode, resolves to the Azure Redis server's hostname.

summary.text: Gets the "id" output reference for the resource.

remarks.text: This property is not available in polyglot app hosts.

summary.text: Gets the "name" output reference for the resource.

remarks.text: This property is not available in polyglot app hosts.

summary.text: Gets the password/access key for the Redis server.
Gets the port for the Redis server.

remarks.text: In container mode, resolves to the container's primary endpoint port. In Azure mode, resolves to 10000.

summary.text: Gets the connection URI expression for the Redis server.

remarks.text: Format:
redis://[:{password}@]{host}:{port}
. The password segment is omitted when using Entra ID authentication in Azure mode.

summary.text: Gets a value indicating whether the resource uses access key authentication.

remarks.text: This property returns null when running in Azure mode, as Redis access is handled via connection strings. When running as a container, it resolves to the password parameter value if one exists.
